channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Does Trump hold ‘all the cards’ against Iran in the Strait of Hormuz?
item.description: Trump signals confidence in Iran standoff as tensions rise in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: India loses its last left-wing government after five decades
item.description: Kerala in India, which gave the world its first democratically elected communist government, has voted the left out.

item.title: Armenia hosts major EU summits in pivot away from Russia
item.description: European leaders gathered in Armenia for two major summits, as security, trade, and the war in Ukraine dominate talks.

item.title: Modi’s party poised for big wins in Indian state elections
item.description: The ruling BJP's lead in two of four key states is expected to significantly weaken the opposition.

item.title: Formula One plans return to V8 engines as electrical revolution loses spark
item.description: F1 cars could return to traditional V8 engines by 2030, FIA President Mohammed Ben Sulayem says at Miami GP.

item.title: Russian strikes kill at least eight in Ukraine, while drones hit Moscow
item.description: Kharkiv and Kherson targeted by missiles, while Russian capital suffers a rare hit from drones.

item.title: Which Premier League teams can still qualify for the Champions League?
item.description: As the Premier League season enters its final games, several teams still dream of entry into Europe's top competition.

item.title: What is hantavirus, suspected in deaths of three people on cruise ship?
item.description: World Health Organization says risk to the general public remains low despite suspected outbreak on the MV Hondius.

item.title: Arsenal vs Atletico Madrid: Champions League – team news, start and lineups
item.description: The two sides, who have never lifted the trophy, renew their rivalry after the first leg of the semifinal finished 1-1.

item.title: War preparations under way in Iran as Hormuz tensions with US escalate
item.description: Amid stalled talks and sparring over key waterway, Iranian media say US warship struck, but Washington denies incident.

item.title: Trump’s ‘Project Freedom’: Can US navy ‘guide’ stuck ships out of Hormuz?
item.description: Trump says US navy will free up ships stranded in the Gulf, but it remains unclear how the campaign will be implemented.

item.title: ‘Hegemonic power’: How Modi’s BJP won India’s Bengal for the first time
item.description: Walking the path of religious polarisation and leveraging underlying anti-incumbency, Modi's party sweeps key state.

item.title: Car show turns to tragedy as monster truck drives into crowd in Colombia
item.description: Distraught onlookers caught the moment a monster truck lost control at a car show and plowed into spectators in Colombia

item.title: ‘Israel is not implementing the ceasefire agreement in Gaza’
item.description: ‘Israel is not implementing the ceasefire agreement in Gaza’

item.title: Tennis stars Sinner, Sabalenka and Gauff criticise Roland Garros prize money
item.description: Players keep up the pressure on the Grand Slam tournaments by asking organisers to increase prize money.

item.title: What is the Ukrainian anti-drone system Sky Map being used in the Gulf?
item.description: Ukraine’s battle-tested system uses thousands of acoustic sensors and interceptors to find and destroy incoming drones.

item.title: Winning peace in Lebanon is harder than winning war
item.description: Resolving the conflict in Lebanon necessitates taking into account its long history and new geopolitical realities.

item.title: Iran says it fired missiles at US warship to prevent it entering Hormuz
item.description: Iranian state media says two missiles have struck a US navy destroyer to prevent it entering the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: NATO chief says Europeans have ‘gotten the message’ from Trump on defence
item.description: The US president has accused some NATO countries of not doing enough to support the US-Israel war on Iran.

item.title: ‘I can’t feel my leg’: Israeli gunfire disables teenagers in West Bank
item.description: Al Jazeera visits refugee camp in the occupied West Bank where Israeli soldiers have killed or maimed many young people.

item.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 66 as Trump announces Hormuz mission?
item.description: Trump orders new Hormuz mission after Iran says it receives US response to its peace proposal.

item.title: Japan PM: Hormuz closure ‘inflicting enormous impact’ on Asia Pacific
item.description: Japan’s PM says the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz is ‘inflicting an ‘enormous impact’ on Asia Pacific.

item.title: Iran warns US to stay out of Hormuz after Trump says US will ‘guide’ ships
item.description: President Trump says the US will begin 'Project Freedom' on Monday to guide stranded ships through Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Crew of Iranian ship seized by US forces evacuated to Pakistan, gov’t says
item.description: Twenty-two crew members from the seized Iranian ship MV Touska have been transferred to Pakistan for repatriation.

item.title: Former NYC Mayor Giuliani hospitalised in ‘critical condition’
item.description: Spokesman says Giuliani in 'critical but stable' condition as President Trump hails 'true warrior'.
